channel.title: Web3 Security Paper Digest
channel.description: Curated papers on Web3, blockchain, and smart contract security from ArXiv and ACL

item.title: TxRay: Agentic Postmortem of Live Blockchain Attacks
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents TxRay, an LLM-driven agentic system that reconstructs Anyone-Can-Take (ACT) DeFi attacks from minimal seed evidence, deriving an evidence-backed root cause and producing runnable, self-contained PoCs.
  • Introduces semantic, executable oracles for self-checking postmortems and PoCEvaluator, an independent agentic execution-and-review evaluator to validate PoC correctness and quality.
  • Evaluated on 114 DeFi incidents: TxRay achieves 92.11% end-to-end reproduction, generates PoCs that mostly avoid hard-coded attacker addresses (98.1%), and delivers median validated root causes in ~40 minutes and PoCs in ~59 minutes.
  • Demonstrates improved attack imitation/coverage over prior tools (STING and APE) and targets DeFi exploit analysis, root-cause attribution, and automated forensic PoC generation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ziyue Wang', 'Jiangshan Yu', 'Kaihua Qin', 'Dawn Song', 'Arthur Gervais', 'Liyi Zhou']
  • Tags: ['smart-contract-security', 'DeFi-exploit-analysis', 'exploit-reconstruction', 'automated-forensics', 'LLM-tools-for-security']

item.title: PoCo: Agentic Proof-of-Concept Exploit Generation for Smart Contracts
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces PoCo, an agentic framework that automatically generates executable proof-of-concept (PoC) exploits for smart contracts from natural-language vulnerability descriptions.
  • Operates in a Reason-Act-Observe loop, interacting with code-execution tools to produce Foundry-compatible, ready-to-run PoC tests.
  • Evaluated on 23 real-world vulnerability reports and shown to outperform Zero-shot and Workflow baselines in producing well-formed, logically correct PoCs.
  • Aims to reduce manual effort in security audits by automating reproducible exploit generation for smart contract vulnerabilities.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Vivi Andersson', 'Sofia Bobadilla', 'Harald Hobbelhagen', 'Martin Monperrus']
  • Tags: ['smart-contracts', 'exploit-generation', 'automated-auditing', 'vulnerability-proof-of-concept', 'agentic-frameworks']
